Aiden and Betty go on Holiday p4: The cottage smelled of sea salt and old wood and something warm that Aiden couldn't name. They raced upstairs to choose rooms. There was a small room and a slightly bigger room. They flipped a coin — Aiden got the bigger room, but Betty got the one with the sea view, which meant it was perfectly even.

Aiden and Betty go on Holiday p4: The cottage smelled of sea salt and old wood and something warm that Aiden couldn't name. They raced upstairs to choose rooms. There was a small room and a slightly bigger room. They flipped a coin — Aiden got the bigger room, but Betty got the one with the sea view, which meant it was perfectly even.

Aiden and Betty go on Holiday p4: The cottage smelled of sea salt and old wood and something warm that Aiden couldn't name. They raced upstairs to choose rooms. There was a small room and a slightly bigger room. They flipped a coin — Aiden got the bigger room, but Betty got the one with the sea view, which meant it was perfectly even.
Flowers and Trees p1: A flower has petals. A flower has a stem. A flower has roots.
Flowers and Trees p5: Trees have roots under the soil. Roots hold the tree up.
Flowers and Trees p5: Trees have roots under the soil. Roots hold the tree up.
James and Anna and Chips p5: James made Chips a lead from a long piece of rope. Now Chips could come on walks!
James and Anna build a Tree House p2: "We should build a tree house!" said James. He got his notebook and drew the plan right away. It had a door, a window, a flag and a rope ladder.
James and Anna build a Tree House p3: Anna made a list. "We will need planks of wood, nails, rope, hinges and paint," she said. "And a flag."

Aiden and Betty have a Yard Sale p9: A girl called Rosa arrived late with her grandmother and spent a thoughtful amount of time choosing. She ended up with three books, a snow globe and the foam sword, which she tucked under her arm like a very satisfied general. Aiden watched the sword go and found he didn't mind at all.
Flowers and Trees p4: A rose is red. It smells sweet. It has sharp thorns.
James and Anna and Chips p7: Then Chips saw a big rose bush. He ran at it. CRUNCH! MUNCH! SNAP! The rose bush was gone.
James and Anna and Chips p7: Then Chips saw a big rose bush. He ran at it. CRUNCH! MUNCH! SNAP! The rose bush was gone.
My Five Senses p6: I can touch. My hands can feel if things are rough or smooth, hot or cold.

Aiden and Betty and the Castle p6: In the armoury, there was a full suit of plate armour. It was smaller than Aiden expected. The guide told them that real knights were roughly the same size as Dad, which was very hard to imagine. Aiden tried on a gauntlet under supervision. His hand barely fit inside.
Shapes Everywhere p1: A circle is round. The sun is a circle. A wheel is a circle.
The Moon p1: Look up at night. You can see the moon! The moon is big and round.
James and Anna go to Space p9: They flew past big round planets. Some had red spots. Some had wide, shining rings!

Aiden and Betty: New Teeth p10: Three weeks later, the new tooth began to come through. It was enormous relative to the gap — a proper square adult tooth in a mouth that had been full of small rounded baby ones. Aiden looked at himself in the mirror and felt extremely strange.

Aiden and Betty go on Holiday p8: On Wednesday it rained all morning. They played three rounds of Cluedo, drank hot chocolate in large mugs and listened to the rain hammer against the cottage windows. It was, Aiden thought, pressing his mug to his chin, one of the cosiest mornings he could remember.

Aiden and Betty: New Teeth p5: Betty lost her first tooth the very next day, which nobody had seen coming. It came out at lunch, and she held it up and examined it with great interest before putting it in her pocket, as if this were a routine scientific procedure.
James and Anna go Shopping p8: Then Anna spotted the cake stand! Big cakes, small cakes, jam tarts and iced buns — all in a long, shining row.
James and Anna build a Tree House p7: Next came the floor — flat planks, all in a row. "Walk on it!" said Dad. They did. It held firm!
James and Anna and the School Play p11: The play went brilliantly after that. James roared and stomped and the little ones in the front row screamed and giggled.

Aiden and Betty have a Yard Sale p6: Their first customer was Mr Perkins from next door, who was very old and took a very long time about everything. He picked up every single item on the table, turned it over carefully, and put it back. After twenty minutes, he held up one small rubber duck. It cost twenty cents.
Tools p5: A ruler measures length.
What is Weather? p6: Rumble! Flash! A storm is here. Then the rain falls.
Dozy's Wonderful Dream p9: At the top of Mount Rumble, all the Dino Pals were there. Sitting on the warm peak. Watching all of Sunny Hollow spread out below, tiny and green and perfect.
The Big Storm p1: The morning felt strange. The air was heavy and still. Mount Rumble was not puffing its usual heart-shaped rings.
Baby Animals p6: A baby horse is a foal. A foal can run fast.
Pets We Love p1: A dog is a pet. Dogs love to play and run.
Pets We Love p6: A hamster is small and fluffy. Hamsters run on a wheel.
Zippy's Race p6: BANG. They were off. Zippy was first. Of course. But the very fast runner was close. Very close.
Zippy's Race p8: Zippy crossed the line first. Just. The second runner arrived a heartbeat later.
Zippy's Race p10: The second runner came over. Good race, they said. You too, said Zippy, breathing hard for the first time ever.
Zippy's Race p1: A message arrived in Sunny Hollow. Zippy delivered it. It took two seconds. There was going to be a race. A real race. With runners from other valleys.
Zippy's Race p5: The race day came. The runners lined up. They were all fast. One was very fast indeed.
James, Anna and Chips go Camping p10: The air horn worked. The fox did not come back. Dad DID come — running from the house in his pyjamas, looking very startled.

The Sunny Hollow Games p2: There would be a running race, a jumping contest, a loudness competition, and a prize for the tidiest corner of the Hollow. More events to be decided, said Bossy. I have a clipboard.
The Sunny Hollow Games p3: Zippy won the running race before anyone else had started. Does it count if no one saw the finish? asked Bossy. It counts, said Zippy.
Bob and Nan's First Day at School p4: Bob is sad.
Bob and Nan's First Day at School p5: Nan is sad.
Fluff Visits the Vet p2: Nan and Bob are sad.
Community Helpers p5: A police officer keeps us safe.
Clothes p6: Clothes keep us safe and warm!
James and Anna go Shopping p6: James found the grapes. Red ones or green ones? He put both bunches in the basket — just to be safe.
James and Anna go to Space p2: "Let us make a rocket!" said James. "We can fly all the way into space!"
James and Anna go to Space p3: Anna grabbed her pen and drew the plan. "It needs big wings," she said, "and a pointed nose cone at the top."
James and Anna go to Space p6: "It is the best rocket ever made!" said James. He and Anna stood back and grinned.
Bouncy Bumps Into Everything p3: MY SAIL! cried Fancy. Sorry! called Bouncy, already bouncing away.
Fancy's Bad Day p1: Fancy woke up and found something terrible. Fancy's sail was bent. Not a little bent. Very bent.
Fancy's Bad Day p4: Chompy offered to sit on the sail to flatten it. ABSOLUTELY NOT, said Fancy.

Aiden and Betty have a Yard Sale p2: "We should have a yard sale," said Betty. She said it the way she said all her best ideas — quickly and firmly, as though the idea had already made up its mind. "We could make actual money." Aiden wasn't certain at first, but when she said the word money, he started to listen.

Aiden and Betty have a Yard Sale p4: Betty made neat round price stickers on square labels, writing each price in her best handwriting. Aiden made the sign for the front gate — YARD SALE: EVERYTHING MUST GO — in six colours, with three exclamation marks, then a fourth one, just to make sure.

Aiden and Betty have a Yard Sale p10: They had set up a lemonade stand beside the sale table — Betty's idea, planned the previous Thursday. Aiden poured from a glass jug. Betty collected the money and kept the queue moving. By one o'clock they had sold every cup and run entirely out of lemons.

Aiden and Betty go on Holiday p4: The cottage smelled of sea salt and old wood and something warm that Aiden couldn't name. They raced upstairs to choose rooms. There was a small room and a slightly bigger room. They flipped a coin — Aiden got the bigger room, but Betty got the one with the sea view, which meant it was perfectly even.

Aiden and Betty go on Holiday p11: On their last evening, they sat on the sea wall with fish and chips wrapped in paper. The chips were crispy and perfectly salty. Aiden told Mum they were the best chips he had ever eaten in his entire life, and Mum said that was the sea air doing it, and she was right.
Magnets p6: Same poles push apart!
James and Anna's New Bikes p8: Then James tried riding with NO hands. He made it exactly three seconds before heading straight into the same bush.
James and Anna's New Bikes p9: Anna sat next to the bush. "Same bush as before," she said. James laughed from somewhere inside it.

Aiden and Betty Start Grade 1 p8: At lunch, they sat together by the window. Aiden opened his lunchbox and found a small folded note from Mum tucked under his sandwich. It said: You've got this, star. He smiled all the way through his apple.

Aiden and Betty: New Teeth p4: Mum said it was completely normal and that they had both lost teeth before when they were smaller — Aiden had even eaten one inside a peanut butter sandwich and not noticed for an hour. Aiden was not certain this was reassuring.
James and Anna's New Bikes p11: They ate their sandwiches by the duck pond. Chips waited at home. Chips and bikes were definitely not a good idea.

Aiden and Betty go on Holiday p5: The beach was a five-minute walk down a steep sandy path through tall marram grass. When they came over the last dune and the sea appeared — wide and green and going all the way to the horizon — Aiden stopped walking entirely and just looked for a long moment.
James and Anna go to the Dentist p11: The dentist sang a little tune while she checked Anna's teeth. Anna smiled the whole time. "Ten out of ten!" said the dentist.
James, Anna and Chips go Camping p12: In the morning, dew was on the grass and the birds sang. Chips had eaten a corner of the tent in the night.
Bob and Nan p6: Bob and Nan sat.
Bob, Nan and Fluff p1: Bob and Nan sat in the sun.
James and Anna go to the Dentist p6: Anna found a book about sharks on the shelf. She sat down and read and read. She forgot to feel scared.

Aiden and Betty and Socks p5: Socks looked at Aiden. Then he looked at Betty. Then he reached out one small black hand, calmly removed Aiden's watch from Aiden's wrist, and put it on his own. He looked at it with great satisfaction. "Charming," said Mum. "Yes," said Uncle Eddie, completely unsurprised.

Aiden and Betty have a Yard Sale p9: A girl called Rosa arrived late with her grandmother and spent a thoughtful amount of time choosing. She ended up with three books, a snow globe and the foam sword, which she tucked under her arm like a very satisfied general. Aiden watched the sword go and found he didn't mind at all.

Aiden and Betty have a Yard Sale p1: It was the first warm Saturday of spring, and Aiden and Betty stood in the middle of the playroom staring at something that had got entirely out of control. Toys, books, games and puzzles were piled up to the shelves. There was even a foam sword that neither of them remembered buying.

Aiden and Betty have a Yard Sale p5: On Saturday morning, they dragged two folding tables out to the front path and arranged everything carefully. The old teddies sat in a row at the back and looked slightly sorry for themselves. "They'll go to good homes," said Aiden, mostly to himself, as a form of reassurance.

Aiden and Betty and Socks p1: It was an ordinary Saturday in late August when Uncle Eddie arrived at the door with a travelling bag, a very wide smile, and something small and determined making a noise from inside his coat.
Tools p2: A saw cuts wood.
Bob, Nan and Fluff p2: Bob saw a pup!
James and Anna and Chips p7: Then Chips saw a big rose bush. He ran at it. CRUNCH! MUNCH! SNAP! The rose bush was gone.
James and Anna go to Space p11: A small green alien ran to say hello. "My name is Zim!" it said. "Do you want to play?"

Aiden and Betty and Socks p4: "His name is Socks," said Uncle Eddie. "He was orphaned when he was very small, and I've raised him from a baby. He understands a lot of what you say, he copies behaviour, and he is completely tame." He paused just a moment. "He is also, just occasionally, a little bit naughty."

Aiden and Betty and Socks p6: Uncle Eddie explained that he was returning to the rainforest for six months and couldn't bring Socks along this time. He looked at Dad with a hopeful expression. Dad said yes before Mum could say anything at all, which was exactly what Mum had expected him to do.

Socks Goes Missing p8: By three o'clock, the sky had gone grey and the wind had picked up. Aiden was sitting on the front step, not saying much. He kept thinking about what Uncle Eddie had said once: that animals raised by hand always know where home is. He held onto that thought very carefully.

Aiden, Betty and Socks's Big Adventure p11: Uncle Eddie left on Sunday evening. He stayed on the doorstep a long time, saying goodbye properly. When the car was gone, Socks sat on the hall windowsill and watched the empty road. Then he came inside and sat on Aiden's lap, which he had never done before. Aiden stayed very still.

Aiden and Betty and the Bully p3: "What happened?" said Betty. Aiden told her in pieces, looking at the floor. Betty listened to all of it without saying anything. Then she said: "He's wrong. And being wrong loudly is still just being wrong." Aiden thought about that.
James and Anna and the School Play p7: On the night of the play, James wore a green scale costume and a red dragon mask. He looked completely brilliant.
Animals in the Ocean p2: A fish has fins. Fins help fish swim. Fish have scales.
James, Anna and Chips go Camping p9: "What if it comes back?" said James. "Then we will scare it," said Anna. She picked up the air horn Dad had packed.
James and Anna go to the Dentist p6: Anna found a book about sharks on the shelf. She sat down and read and read. She forgot to feel scared.
Zippy's Race p7: Zippy ran harder. The scarf was flat out behind. Everything was a blur.
Sneezy and the Waterfall p11: AAAAAAACHOOOOOO! The sneeze hit the rocks like a wave. Rocks scattered in every direction. The stream burst free. The waterfall roared back. A rainbow appeared.
James and Anna and the School Play p3: Anna did not want to be the dragon. She wanted to paint the big backdrop — the scenery behind the stage.
Bob and Nan's First Day at School p7: Bob and Nan had fun at school!
James and Anna and the School Play p1: Mrs. Green told the class: "We are putting on a school play! It is called 'The Lost Dragon'."
James and Anna and the School Play p8: Then the lights went up and James walked on stage. He could see the WHOLE school in the dark. His legs went very wobbly.
Things That Float and Sink p7: Some things float. Some things sink. Science is fun!

Aiden and Betty Start Grade 1 p11: Then there was Science — real Science, with a magnifying glass and a tray of soil and seeds. Aiden forgot to be nervous. He was too busy looking at a woodlouse through the magnifying glass and writing down exactly what he saw.

Aiden and Betty Start Grade 1 p11: Then there was Science — real Science, with a magnifying glass and a tray of soil and seeds. Aiden forgot to be nervous. He was too busy looking at a woodlouse through the magnifying glass and writing down exactly what he saw.

Aiden and Betty and the Science Fair p2: "What shall we study?" said Betty. They sat with the notebook between them and listed every idea they had. Aiden had eleven. Betty had nine. Three of them were exactly the same, which Betty said proved they had good scientific minds.

Aiden and Betty: New Teeth p5: Betty lost her first tooth the very next day, which nobody had seen coming. It came out at lunch, and she held it up and examined it with great interest before putting it in her pocket, as if this were a routine scientific procedure.
Wiggly's Messy Day p8: Wiggly smiled. Current score, said Wiggly. Tail: twelve. Wiggly: also twelve.
James and Anna and the School Play p11: The play went brilliantly after that. James roared and stomped and the little ones in the front row screamed and giggled.
Animals in the Ocean p8: So many animals live in the sea! The ocean is amazing.

Aiden and Betty go on Holiday p4: The cottage smelled of sea salt and old wood and something warm that Aiden couldn't name. They raced upstairs to choose rooms. There was a small room and a slightly bigger room. They flipped a coin — Aiden got the bigger room, but Betty got the one with the sea view, which meant it was perfectly even.

Aiden and Betty go on Holiday p4: The cottage smelled of sea salt and old wood and something warm that Aiden couldn't name. They raced upstairs to choose rooms. There was a small room and a slightly bigger room. They flipped a coin — Aiden got the bigger room, but Betty got the one with the sea view, which meant it was perfectly even.
Animals in the Ocean p6: A seahorse is tiny. It holds on to plants.

Socks Goes Missing p3: They searched everywhere. Aiden checked under every sofa cushion and behind every bookshelf. Betty methodically looked in every high cupboard. Mum turned the garden upside down. Dad searched the garden again, more systematically, with a torch — even though it was daytime. Socks was not anywhere.

Socks Goes Missing p3: They searched everywhere. Aiden checked under every sofa cushion and behind every bookshelf. Betty methodically looked in every high cupboard. Mum turned the garden upside down. Dad searched the garden again, more systematically, with a torch — even though it was daytime. Socks was not anywhere.
Clumsy to the Rescue p2: Everyone searched. Bouncy bounced into every corner. Zippy ran everywhere twice. Cheeky looked in all the hiding spots. Nothing.
Seasons p5: The seasons change.
Seasons p6: I like all four seasons!
The Four Seasons p9: Four seasons: spring, summer, autumn, winter. Which do you like best?
James, Anna and Chips go Camping p2: James and Anna helped bang in the tent pegs and thread the poles through. Chips ate one peg. Then he tried to eat a second.

Aiden and Betty and the Science Fair p3: They decided to grow crystals. It was Betty's original idea and Aiden's second choice, which Betty said made it the obvious answer. They ordered the kit and spent four days watching for the post.

Aiden and Betty and the Bully p4: That night, Aiden wrote in his notebook: When something is unfair, you have two choices — pretend it isn't happening, or decide what to do. He starred the second option. He didn't quite know yet what doing something looked like.
James and Anna's New Bikes p8: Then James tried riding with NO hands. He made it exactly three seconds before heading straight into the same bush.

Aiden, Betty and Socks's Big Adventure p1: Uncle Eddie came back for a long weekend in June — browner from the sun, with a new bag of unusual gifts and more stories than they had time to hear. Socks heard the doorbell and ran in circles for forty-five seconds without stopping.
Zippy's Race p1: A message arrived in Sunny Hollow. Zippy delivered it. It took two seconds. There was going to be a race. A real race. With runners from other valleys.

Aiden and Betty and the Castle p11: In the castle shop, Betty bought a small accurate model of the castle as it looked in 1350, before a section of wall fell. Aiden bought a book on medieval building techniques. Dad bought a novelty ceramic goblet that Mum said, quietly but firmly, would not be coming into the kitchen.
The Big Storm p9: Bouncy bounced up to reach the high branches and secure anything at risk. Only bumped into three things. A new record, said Bouncy.
The Sun p4: The sun helps us see.
Colors p6: I see all the colors!
Five Senses p1: I see with my eyes.
How Things Grow p3: A seed grows into a plant.
Little Seeds Grow p1: This is a seed. It is tiny. Seeds come in many shapes.
Little Seeds Grow p2: Put the seed in soil. Pat it down gently.
Little Seeds Grow p1: This is a seed. It is tiny. Seeds come in many shapes.
James and Anna go Shopping p5: Anna ran to find the bread. She picked the big round loaf with seeds on top. It smelled amazing!
James and Anna visit Grandma's Farm p4: Anna found the duck pond. Three white ducks swam in slow circles. One waddled right up and ate seeds from Anna's hand.

Aiden and Betty Start Grade 1 p6: The first lesson was handwriting — joined-up letters, which were completely new and seemed designed to be tricky. Aiden's pen slipped and he made a large blot right across the middle of the page. He went very red.

Aiden and Betty and Socks p7: Socks explored the house in eleven minutes. He opened every drawer he could reach, rearranged the fruit bowl into a different order, sat briefly on each windowsill and posted three letters from the hall table into the back of the television. He seemed very pleased with his work.

Aiden and Betty: New Teeth p12: Socks was fascinated by both of them through this whole period. He kept trying to look in their mouths. He seemed to find it deeply interesting, which Aiden added to the field notebook under: Primate-specific dental curiosity — possible.
The Sunny Hollow Games p10: Fancy held a Best Sail competition. Fancy was the only one with a sail. Fancy gave themselves first prize. This seems fair, said Fancy.

Aiden and Betty go on Holiday p10: The view from the top was unlike anything Aiden had seen. The whole coastline curved away in both directions — bays and headlands, a small fishing village far below, and the sea stretching to the horizon, catching the light like crinkled silver foil.

Aiden and Betty and Socks p3: Carefully, Uncle Eddie unzipped the carry-case. Inside, curled up like a small comma, was the most compact monkey Aiden had ever seen. He had cream-white fur on his face and chest, darker brown on his back, and small black hands and feet that looked, unmistakably, like he was wearing a pair of extremely neat little socks.

Aiden and Betty and the Science Fair p12: That evening, Aiden added a new page to his field notebook: WHAT MAKES A GOOD EXPERIMENT. He had six points. Socks sat on the desk and watched him write, which Aiden had never seen him do before.
Nan and Bob go to the Zoo p2: Bob sees a big cat.
Nan and Bob go to the Zoo p3: Nan sees a red bird.
Nan and Bob go to the Zoo p5: Nan sees a fat frog on a log.

Aiden and Betty have a Yard Sale p3: They spent Friday evening sorting everything into three piles: SELL, KEEP and MAYBE. The MAYBE pile kept losing things to SELL, because, as Betty pointed out, they hadn't touched any of them in over a year. By nine o'clock, the MAYBE pile had completely disappeared.

Aiden and Betty have a Yard Sale p3: They spent Friday evening sorting everything into three piles: SELL, KEEP and MAYBE. The MAYBE pile kept losing things to SELL, because, as Betty pointed out, they hadn't touched any of them in over a year. By nine o'clock, the MAYBE pile had completely disappeared.

Aiden and Betty have a Yard Sale p7: Then things got busy. Children came from further up the street with their parents. A woman bought a whole crate of craft supplies without looking at any of them. A small boy bought four plastic dinosaurs and a jigsaw puzzle and carried them away in a tight proud bundle. Things were selling faster than Betty could write them down.

Aiden and Betty have a Yard Sale p13: As Aiden folded up the last table, he found something wedged at the very back — his old stuffed elephant, Nelly, who he had completely forgotten was there. He held her for a long moment. Then he carried her inside and put her on the shelf above his desk. Some things, he decided, were not for selling.
My Five Senses p8: Which sense do you like best?

Aiden and Betty and the Bully p1: There was a boy in Year 2 called Marcus who thought it was funny to call people names. He had decided, for reasons that made no sense at all, that Aiden's name was Lazy-den. He said it on Wednesday. Two children laughed, which made him say it again on Thursday.
Five Senses p6: My five senses help me learn!
Our Five Senses p6: Our senses help us explore!
My Five Senses p1: I have five senses. They help me know the world.
James and Anna build a Tree House p13: Mum sent up a tray of juice and biscuits on a rope. Dad waved from below. Chips jangled at the base of the tree, trying to reach the biscuits.

Socks Goes Missing p5: They went out onto the pavement and called his name. "Socks! Socks!" The October wind moved through the trees and sent leaves skittering across the path. No small monkey appeared. Aiden put his hands in his pockets and tried not to feel too worried. It didn't work very well.

The Sunny Hollow Games p11: Sneezy entered the farthest-blast competition. Sneezy's sneeze sent the test leaf all the way to the Long Meadow. The judge had to run to collect the result. Zippy collected it. Obviously.

Aiden and Betty and the Science Fair p1: Miss Okafor announced the Science Fair on a grey Wednesday in February. Every pair in Grade 1 would present a project. Aiden had his notebook open before she had finished the sentence.

Aiden, Betty and Socks's Big Adventure p6: Socks had other interests. He had crossed a wide fallen tree over the river before anyone had finished the sentence. Aiden said they should follow. Uncle Eddie said absolutely not. Then Uncle Eddie looked at the tree. Then they crossed it.
Clumsy to the Rescue p1: Has anyone seen my pillow? said Dozy. Dozy had been looking for it all morning. This was serious.

Aiden and Betty and Socks p11: Over the following weeks, Socks wove himself into the house. He had his own peg for his waistcoat. He met them at the door when they came home from school. He sat on Dad's shoulder while Dad read the newspaper and turned the pages for him, very seriously.
James and Anna and Chips p6: They set off down the lane. Chips trotted along and stopped to munch the long grass at the sides.
James and Anna's New Bikes p3: James set off first. He got going — then wobbled — then wobbled more — then crashed softly into the hedge. He was fine. Just surprised.
James and Anna's New Bikes p4: Anna set off nice and slowly. She did not wobble. She did not crash. She rode all the way to the end of the path and back.

Aiden and Betty and Socks p13: "He is completely impossible," said Mum, as she did most evenings. But she had also started setting a small dish of whatever she was cooking on the corner of the counter every night, because Socks knew exactly where it appeared and waited there very patiently, and she had not been able to bring herself to stop.

Aiden and Betty and Socks p10: That night, Dad built Socks a cosy sleeping box — a wooden crate lined with a soft fleece blanket — and settled it in the warm corner of the kitchen. In the morning, Socks was not in it. He was curled up fast asleep at the foot of Aiden's bed, his small black hand curled around Aiden's pencil torch.

Aiden and Betty and the Bully p12: Miss Okafor had noticed, in the way that good teachers always notice. She started a class discussion about what it means to be fair, and what you do when things aren't. Several children looked at the floor. Marcus looked at the window.
Honky's Inside Voice p7: IT IS OKAY! called Honky. Honky's voice was louder than the thunder. Dozy stopped shaking.

Aiden and Betty and the Bully p8: It was quiet. Nobody laughed. Marcus said something low, but nobody around him responded. Aiden walked on. His legs were slightly shaky — but he hadn't shown it, and he was extremely proud of himself.

Aiden and Betty have a Yard Sale p11: "What shall we spend it on?" Betty asked. They sat on the front step with the money tin between them and talked it through the way they talked through important things — slowly and carefully, considering every option.

Aiden and Betty and the Science Fair p2: "What shall we study?" said Betty. They sat with the notebook between them and listed every idea they had. Aiden had eleven. Betty had nine. Three of them were exactly the same, which Betty said proved they had good scientific minds.

Aiden, Betty and Socks's Big Adventure p8: He led them to a shallow pool in a mossy clearing — fed by a spring, perfectly clear and still. Two dragonflies skimmed the surface, electric blue. Aiden crouched at the edge and looked in. Small things moved in the shallows. He forgot to breathe.

Aiden, Betty and Socks's Big Adventure p8: He led them to a shallow pool in a mossy clearing — fed by a spring, perfectly clear and still. Two dragonflies skimmed the surface, electric blue. Aiden crouched at the edge and looked in. Small things moved in the shallows. He forgot to breathe.
The Moon p3: The moon changes shape. Sometimes it looks like a banana.
Chompy and Grumpy's Day Out p6: Grumpy sat. It was the right temperature. The right shape. Grumpy almost smiled.
Shapes p6: Shapes are all around!
Little Seeds Grow p1: This is a seed. It is tiny. Seeds come in many shapes.
Shapes Everywhere p7: Shapes are everywhere! Can you find shapes today?
Chompy's Big Lunch p4: Sunny shared her lunch. Here you go, Chompy! said Sunny.
James and Anna go to the Dentist p6: Anna found a book about sharks on the shelf. She sat down and read and read. She forgot to feel scared.
Flowers and Trees p4: A rose is red. It smells sweet. It has sharp thorns.
James and Anna go to Space p3: Anna grabbed her pen and drew the plan. "It needs big wings," she said, "and a pointed nose cone at the top."
James and Anna and Chips p8: "Oh, Chips!" said Anna. But she gave him a big hug. Chips licked her right on the nose.
James and Anna go Shopping p1: It was a bright autumn day. Mum had a long list. "I need help at the shop," she said. "Can you both come?"

Aiden and Betty go on Holiday p12: Betty found a small smooth white pebble at the shoreline and turned it over and over in her fingers all the way back to the cottage. She was going to put it on her windowsill at home, so she'd always have a piece of the coast — something real from a real day.
Baby Animals p5: A baby sheep is a lamb. A lamb has soft wool.
Animals on the Farm p4: A sheep has thick wool. Wool keeps us warm.
James and Anna go to the Dentist p6: Anna found a book about sharks on the shelf. She sat down and read and read. She forgot to feel scared.

Aiden and Betty have a Yard Sale p13: As Aiden folded up the last table, he found something wedged at the very back — his old stuffed elephant, Nelly, who he had completely forgotten was there. He held her for a long moment. Then he carried her inside and put her on the shelf above his desk. Some things, he decided, were not for selling.

Aiden and Betty and the Science Fair p5: Then Socks found the crystal jar. He did not eat it. He simply moved it from the windowsill to a high shelf, where he sat beside it with the expression of someone who had just acquired something important.
Reptiles p4: Turtles have a hard shell.
Animals in the Ocean p5: A crab has a hard shell. A crab walks sideways.
Bugs All Around Us p7: A snail has a shell on its back. A snail moves very slowly.

Aiden and Betty have a Yard Sale p1: It was the first warm Saturday of spring, and Aiden and Betty stood in the middle of the playroom staring at something that had got entirely out of control. Toys, books, games and puzzles were piled up to the shelves. There was even a foam sword that neither of them remembered buying.
Honky's Inside Voice p2: Shh! said Grumpy. SORRY! said Honky. More leaves fell.
Day and Night p4: The moon and stars come out. They shine in the dark sky.
Little Seeds Grow p4: The sun shines. Something is happening under the soil!
The Moon p2: The moon shines at night. It gives us light in the dark.
Day and Night p1: In the day, the sun shines. The sky is bright and blue.
James and Anna go to Space p9: They flew past big round planets. Some had red spots. Some had wide, shining rings!
James and Anna go Shopping p8: Then Anna spotted the cake stand! Big cakes, small cakes, jam tarts and iced buns — all in a long, shining row.

Aiden and Betty and Socks p8: "He loves shiny objects," Uncle Eddie explained at dinner. "He adores warm spots — the airing cupboard is a particular risk. And if he gets bored, he will find a way to become interesting." Everyone filed this information away carefully.

Socks Goes Missing p7: Aiden re-read his field notebook carefully, looking for anything useful. He had written: Drawn to shiny objects. Drawn to warm spots. Will follow interesting smells. Exceptionally good at getting into places he shouldn't. He stared at that last line for quite a long time. It did not narrow things down.

Socks Goes Missing p10: They ran. All four of them, in their socks, without stopping to find shoes, down the pavement to number thirty-seven. They could hear, even from the gate, a small and very determined crunching sound coming from inside.
James and Anna go to Space p5: James got the red paint. Anna got the silver paint. They painted and painted until it shone!
James and Anna build a Tree House p14: That night, they got their sleeping bags and climbed up to sleep. Stars shone above them. "Best. Night. Ever," said James. Anna smiled. She agreed.
James, Anna and Chips go Camping p8: At midnight, a fox barked in the lane. Chips went very still. James shone the torch outside. Nothing was there.
James and Anna and the School Play p4: James practised his dragon roar all week. It shook the windows. Mum wore earmuffs. Chips ran under the sofa.
Little Seeds Grow p5: A green shoot comes up! It has two small leaves.
James and Anna go Shopping p1: It was a bright autumn day. Mum had a long list. "I need help at the shop," she said. "Can you both come?"
James and Anna go Shopping p3: The shop was big and bright. It smelled of fresh bread and ripe fruit.

Aiden and Betty and the Castle p11: In the castle shop, Betty bought a small accurate model of the castle as it looked in 1350, before a section of wall fell. Aiden bought a book on medieval building techniques. Dad bought a novelty ceramic goblet that Mum said, quietly but firmly, would not be coming into the kitchen.
James and Anna go Shopping p11: At the till, Mum let James put the shopping on the belt. He was VERY careful with the eggs.

Aiden and Betty go on Holiday p7: Betty was brilliant at boogie boarding from the very first wave. She rode it all the way to shore, fell off, laughed, and waded back in immediately. By the afternoon she was steering around in the foam. Dad tried it once and fell off at once, which everyone found funny, including Dad.

Aiden and Betty go on Holiday p12: Betty found a small smooth white pebble at the shoreline and turned it over and over in her fingers all the way back to the cottage. She was going to put it on her windowsill at home, so she'd always have a piece of the coast — something real from a real day.
Shapes Everywhere p4: A rectangle has four sides. Two sides are long, two are short. A door is a rectangle. A book is a rectangle.

Socks Goes Missing p9: Then the phone rang. It was their neighbour Mrs Obi, from number thirty-seven, three houses down. Her voice was very precise. "I believe," she said carefully, "that there may be a small monkey in my kitchen. He appears to be eating my shortbread."

Socks Goes Missing p11: There was Socks, sitting on Mrs Obi's kitchen counter beside an open shortbread tin. He was wearing his waistcoat — which they had also assumed was missing — and had crumbs on his face and the expression of someone who sees absolutely no problem with any of this whatsoever.

Socks Goes Missing p12: "How on earth did he get in?" asked Dad. Mrs Obi pointed to the cat flap in her back door. Of course. Socks had worked out the cat flap. He had also — from the state of the shortbread tin — clearly been in before, which nobody had known and which explained certain things about Mrs Obi's biscuit situation.
James and Anna go to Space p8: Up, up, up went the rocket! They shot past the white clouds and into the deep, dark sky.
James and Anna build a Tree House p2: "We should build a tree house!" said James. He got his notebook and drew the plan right away. It had a door, a window, a flag and a rope ladder.

Aiden and Betty have a Yard Sale p2: "We should have a yard sale," said Betty. She said it the way she said all her best ideas — quickly and firmly, as though the idea had already made up its mind. "We could make actual money." Aiden wasn't certain at first, but when she said the word money, he started to listen.

Socks Goes Missing p14: On the way home, Betty said they needed a tracker collar. Aiden said they needed to block every cat flap on the street. Mum said they needed a more interesting biscuit tin. Dad said they should call Uncle Eddie and tell him what his monkey had been up to. They all laughed, and kept laughing, all the way home — and Socks rode on Aiden's shoulder the whole way, looking, as always, entirely pleased with himself.

Aiden and Betty go on Holiday p3: The drive took three hours. Betty counted every red car they passed. Aiden read two chapters of his book, fell asleep on his own shoulder, woke up, read another chapter, and then asked "Are we nearly there?" for the fourth time. They were, finally, nearly there.

Aiden and Betty and Socks p11: Over the following weeks, Socks wove himself into the house. He had his own peg for his waistcoat. He met them at the door when they came home from school. He sat on Dad's shoulder while Dad read the newspaper and turned the pages for him, very seriously.

Socks Goes Missing p14: On the way home, Betty said they needed a tracker collar. Aiden said they needed to block every cat flap on the street. Mum said they needed a more interesting biscuit tin. Dad said they should call Uncle Eddie and tell him what his monkey had been up to. They all laughed, and kept laughing, all the way home — and Socks rode on Aiden's shoulder the whole way, looking, as always, entirely pleased with himself.

Socks Goes Missing p7: Aiden re-read his field notebook carefully, looking for anything useful. He had written: Drawn to shiny objects. Drawn to warm spots. Will follow interesting smells. Exceptionally good at getting into places he shouldn't. He stared at that last line for quite a long time. It did not narrow things down.
Fancy's Bad Day p7: Honky offered to shout at it until it moved. Everyone agreed this would make things much worse.

Aiden and Betty go on Holiday p1: The last day of the summer term was also the loudest day of the school year. Aiden and Betty burst through the front door and both shouted "HOLIDAY!" at exactly the same moment, which echoed all the way up the stairs and made the dog next door bark.

Aiden and Betty Start Grade 1 p7: Betty leaned over and showed him her page quietly. Her joined-up writing was also not her best work. "Mine looks like a wonky caterpillar," she whispered. Aiden laughed before he could stop himself, and his face stopped being red.

Aiden and Betty: New Teeth p13: "I think he'll be disappointed when we stop changing," said Aiden. "He already has all the teeth he'll get," said Betty. Socks demonstrated this by grinning at them both from very close range, showing every single tooth.

Aiden and Betty and Socks p14: Aiden had been keeping a field notebook — just as Uncle Eddie had shown him. He had titled it: Socks: A Field Study. He had written things like: Prefers grapes to apple. Expresses disapproval by sitting with back turned. Unusually precise about the order of objects. He turned to a fresh page. It was going to take a very long time to fully understand Socks. He hoped, quietly, that he had the time.

Aiden and Betty and the Bully p8: It was quiet. Nobody laughed. Marcus said something low, but nobody around him responded. Aiden walked on. His legs were slightly shaky — but he hadn't shown it, and he was extremely proud of himself.

Socks Goes Missing p4: "He couldn't have got out," said Dad. "All the windows were shut last night." Then Mum remembered the bathroom window — the small one above the bath, left open just a crack for steam. She looked at Aiden and Aiden looked at Betty. A crack that was, they all realised at the same moment, almost exactly the width of a small monkey.
Shy's Secret Gift p10: Shy! said Sunny, softly. The gifts are from you, aren't they? Shy nodded, going very pink.
Shy's Secret Gift p10: Shy! said Sunny, softly. The gifts are from you, aren't they? Shy nodded, going very pink.
Shy's Secret Gift p11: They were the best gifts we ever had, said Sunny. Will you come and sit with us? Shy came out. Just a little way. But it was something.
Community Helpers p2: A doctor helps sick people.
James and Anna and the School Play p9: He forgot his first line. The hall went silent. Anna was watching from the side. She caught his eye and gave him a thumbs up.
Chips's Play Date p9: At last, both Chips and Bella lay down on the lawn side by side, panting. Chips licked Bella's ear. Bella licked Chips's nose.
Chips's Play Date p9: At last, both Chips and Bella lay down on the lawn side by side, panting. Chips licked Bella's ear. Bella licked Chips's nose.
Shapes Everywhere p2: A square has four equal sides. A window is a square. A cracker is a square.
Shapes Everywhere p3: A triangle has three sides. A pizza slice is a triangle. A roof is a triangle.
Shapes Everywhere p4: A rectangle has four sides. Two sides are long, two are short. A door is a rectangle. A book is a rectangle.
Animals in the Ocean p5: A crab has a hard shell. A crab walks sideways.
Honky's Inside Voice p4: Honky tried very hard to be quiet. Hello, whispered Honky. It still blew Chompy's bib sideways.
Chompy and Grumpy's Day Out p8: Grumpy looked at Chompy in the ditch. Grumpy looked at the perfect flat rock. Grumpy sighed. Hold on.

Aiden and Betty and the Castle p10: On the way down, Aiden stopped at a narrow slit window in the tower wall — an arrow loop. He looked through it. The angle was exact — a perfect line of sight across the approach. Everything in this building was designed for a specific purpose.
Shapes p2: A sign is a square.

Aiden and Betty have a Yard Sale p4: Betty made neat round price stickers on square labels, writing each price in her best handwriting. Aiden made the sign for the front gate — YARD SALE: EVERYTHING MUST GO — in six colours, with three exclamation marks, then a fourth one, just to make sure.

Aiden and Betty: New Teeth p8: He held it up for a long time. It was tiny. It was the smallest thing that had ever felt significant. He put it in the small envelope Mum left for him, and when he placed it under his pillow it felt ceremonial.
James and Anna and the School Play p9: He forgot his first line. The hall went silent. Anna was watching from the side. She caught his eye and gave him a thumbs up.
James and Anna and Chips p12: "CHIPS!" they both said. But they could not stop smiling. Chips was naughty and cheeky and silly — and they loved him so much.
James and Anna go to Space p5: James got the red paint. Anna got the silver paint. They painted and painted until it shone!

Aiden and Betty go on Holiday p10: The view from the top was unlike anything Aiden had seen. The whole coastline curved away in both directions — bays and headlands, a small fishing village far below, and the sea stretching to the horizon, catching the light like crinkled silver foil.

Aiden and Betty and the Science Fair p5: Then Socks found the crystal jar. He did not eat it. He simply moved it from the windowsill to a high shelf, where he sat beside it with the expression of someone who had just acquired something important.

Aiden and Betty: New Teeth p11: "You don't look strange," said Betty. "You look older." She said it the way she said true things — simply, as though it were just a fact. Aiden looked in the mirror again. It was going to take some getting used to.

Aiden and Betty go on Holiday p2: They were going to the coast for a whole week — a small blue cottage right on the clifftop, with a gate in the garden wall that opened straight onto the coastal path. Mum had booked it in January. They had been counting down ever since.

Aiden and Betty and the Castle p1: Dad had been talking about the castle trip since January. A proper medieval castle, forty minutes away — with a tower you could actually climb, a dungeon that was a real dungeon, and a great hall whose stone floor had been there for six hundred years. Betty had already read a book about it.
The Four Seasons p1: This is spring. Flowers pop up. Birds sing.
Day and Night p7: Day, then night, then day again. Every single day!
James, Anna and Chips go Camping p4: Chips was coming too. He had his own little blanket and his bell, which jingled every single time he moved.

Aiden and Betty have a Yard Sale p6: Their first customer was Mr Perkins from next door, who was very old and took a very long time about everything. He picked up every single item on the table, turned it over carefully, and put it back. After twenty minutes, he held up one small rubber duck. It cost twenty cents.
Things That Float and Sink p5: A big rock is heavy. Heavy things often sink.
Things That Float and Sink p7: Some things float. Some things sink. Science is fun!
Things That Float and Sink p2: Drop a stone in water. It sinks to the bottom.
Things That Float and Sink p3: A leaf floats. A coin sinks.
Things That Float and Sink p6: Try it yourself! What floats? What sinks?
Nan and Bob get Sick p6: Nan and Bob sip and rest.
Bob and Nan's First Day at School p6: Bob and Nan sit at a desk.
Chips's Play Date p8: Mrs. Chen stood at the garden gate and laughed so hard that she had to sit down on the grass.
Shy's Secret Gift p11: They were the best gifts we ever had, said Sunny. Will you come and sit with us? Shy came out. Just a little way. But it was something.
Fluff Visits the Vet p4: Fluff sits on the big bed.

Aiden and Betty and Socks p14: Aiden had been keeping a field notebook — just as Uncle Eddie had shown him. He had titled it: Socks: A Field Study. He had written things like: Prefers grapes to apple. Expresses disapproval by sitting with back turned. Unusually precise about the order of objects. He turned to a fresh page. It was going to take a very long time to fully understand Socks. He hoped, quietly, that he had the time.

Socks Goes Missing p8: By three o'clock, the sky had gone grey and the wind had picked up. Aiden was sitting on the front step, not saying much. He kept thinking about what Uncle Eddie had said once: that animals raised by hand always know where home is. He held onto that thought very carefully.

Socks Goes Missing p11: There was Socks, sitting on Mrs Obi's kitchen counter beside an open shortbread tin. He was wearing his waistcoat — which they had also assumed was missing — and had crumbs on his face and the expression of someone who sees absolutely no problem with any of this whatsoever.

Socks Goes Missing p12: "How on earth did he get in?" asked Dad. Mrs Obi pointed to the cat flap in her back door. Of course. Socks had worked out the cat flap. He had also — from the state of the shortbread tin — clearly been in before, which nobody had known and which explained certain things about Mrs Obi's biscuit situation.
Nan and Bob: Bob's Birthday Party p5: Bob can see six big candles.
James and Anna and the School Play p2: James put his hand straight up. "I want to be the dragon!" Six other children put their hands up too. They would all have to try out.

Aiden and Betty have a Yard Sale p4: Betty made neat round price stickers on square labels, writing each price in her best handwriting. Aiden made the sign for the front gate — YARD SALE: EVERYTHING MUST GO — in six colours, with three exclamation marks, then a fourth one, just to make sure.

Aiden and Betty and the Science Fair p7: They made their display board on a rainy Saturday — a bright blue background, a photograph of each day's crystal growth, Betty's data chart, and a title in Aiden's best six-colour lettering: HOW DO CRYSTALS GROW?

Aiden and Betty and the Castle p6: In the armoury, there was a full suit of plate armour. It was smaller than Aiden expected. The guide told them that real knights were roughly the same size as Dad, which was very hard to imagine. Aiden tried on a gauntlet under supervision. His hand barely fit inside.

Aiden, Betty and Socks's Big Adventure p8: He led them to a shallow pool in a mossy clearing — fed by a spring, perfectly clear and still. Two dragonflies skimmed the surface, electric blue. Aiden crouched at the edge and looked in. Small things moved in the shallows. He forgot to breathe.
Reptiles p1: Reptiles have dry skin.
Our Five Senses p5: We use our skin to touch.
The Four Seasons p2: In spring, baby animals are born. A lamb skips. A chick hatches.

Socks Goes Missing p5: They went out onto the pavement and called his name. "Socks! Socks!" The October wind moved through the trees and sent leaves skittering across the path. No small monkey appeared. Aiden put his hands in his pockets and tried not to feel too worried. It didn't work very well.
Colors p2: The sky is blue.
Day and Night p1: The sun is in the sky.
Day and Night p3: The moon is in the sky.
Day and Night p4: We sleep at night.
Clothes p5: We wear pajamas to sleep.
The Four Seasons p8: In winter, some animals sleep. We wrap up warm.
Wiggly's Messy Day p7: I like your tail, said Dozy sleepily. It fans me while I nap. The Dino Pals all agreed.
James and Anna build a Tree House p14: That night, they got their sleeping bags and climbed up to sleep. Stars shone above them. "Best. Night. Ever," said James. Anna smiled. She agreed.
James, Anna and Chips go Camping p3: They packed their sleeping bags, a torch, a pack of cards, two books and lots of snacks.

Aiden and Betty and Socks p10: That night, Dad built Socks a cosy sleeping box — a wooden crate lined with a soft fleece blanket — and settled it in the warm corner of the kitchen. In the morning, Socks was not in it. He was curled up fast asleep at the foot of Aiden's bed, his small black hand curled around Aiden's pencil torch.
Animal Homes p2: A bear sleeps in a cave.
James and Anna visit Grandma's Farm p12: They sat outside as the sun went down. Bess looked over her gate. Percy slept in the mud. Chips ate the fence post. It had been the best farm day ever.

The Sunny Hollow Games p9: Dozy slept through most of the events and woke up in time for the picnic. Did I win anything? said Dozy. Most restful competitor, said Bossy. Deserved, said Dozy, and fell asleep again.
Shapes Everywhere p3: A triangle has three sides. A pizza slice is a triangle. A roof is a triangle.
James and Anna go Shopping p10: James chose a big slice of lemon cake with thick white icing. Anna chose a pink strawberry jam tart.
James and Anna and Chips p9: They got home just in time for lunch. James filled Chips's bowl with fresh grass and sliced apple.
James and Anna and the School Play p5: On the day of try-outs, James roared so loud that Mrs. Green's glasses slid right down her nose. He got the part!

Aiden and Betty: New Teeth p1: It started with a slight wobble. Aiden was eating an apple when he felt, for the first time in his life, that one of his front teeth was moving in a way that front teeth were definitely not supposed to move.

Aiden and Betty Start Grade 1 p4: "In Grade 1," said Miss Okafor, "we will read chapter books, solve number puzzles and begin to understand how the world around us works." Aiden thought that sounded like an awful lot — but he found he was leaning forward slightly without meaning to.

Aiden and Betty Start Grade 1 p5: Everyone had their own desk with a name label. Aiden's said AIDEN in neat blue letters. He ran his finger over it slowly and felt, just slightly, like it might turn out to be all right.

Aiden and Betty Start Grade 1 p9: After lunch came Maths. Miss Okafor wrote sums on the board, each one slightly harder than the last. Some of them made Aiden's brain feel stretched in an unfamiliar way. He was discovering that this was not entirely a bad feeling.

Aiden and Betty Start Grade 1 p6: The first lesson was handwriting — joined-up letters, which were completely new and seemed designed to be tricky. Aiden's pen slipped and he made a large blot right across the middle of the page. He went very red.
Cheeky's Prank Goes Wrong p5: Cheeky laughed and laughed. But then Cheeky slipped on the same mud. SPLAT!

Aiden and Betty and the Castle p10: On the way down, Aiden stopped at a narrow slit window in the tower wall — an arrow loop. He looked through it. The angle was exact — a perfect line of sight across the approach. Everything in this building was designed for a specific purpose.

Aiden, Betty and Socks's Big Adventure p4: Betty found the bird hide first — a long wooden structure with narrow slot windows over the reed bed. Inside: a log book, borrowed binoculars, and a board listing twelve species to find. She was immediately in her element.
James and Anna visit Grandma's Farm p4: Anna found the duck pond. Three white ducks swam in slow circles. One waddled right up and ate seeds from Anna's hand.
Bugs All Around Us p7: A snail has a shell on its back. A snail moves very slowly.
The Moon p5: The moon goes around the Earth. It travels very slowly.
James and Anna's New Bikes p4: Anna set off nice and slowly. She did not wobble. She did not crash. She rode all the way to the end of the path and back.
Animals at Night p4: A hedgehog snuffles in the dark. It looks for slugs and bugs.
Little Seeds Grow p5: A green shoot comes up! It has two small leaves.
Flowers and Trees p3: A daisy is small and white. It grows in the grass.
Bugs All Around Us p1: Bugs are all around us. Big bugs. Small bugs. Look closely!

Aiden and Betty: New Teeth p4: Mum said it was completely normal and that they had both lost teeth before when they were smaller — Aiden had even eaten one inside a peanut butter sandwich and not noticed for an hour. Aiden was not certain this was reassuring.

Aiden and Betty and the Castle p3: The entrance was a proper drawbridge — working, over a real water-filled moat. It was smaller than he'd imagined and more real than he'd imagined. Aiden crossed it one step at a time, listening to the echo of his footsteps on old wood, thinking about every person who had crossed it before him.

Aiden and Betty and the Castle p6: In the armoury, there was a full suit of plate armour. It was smaller than Aiden expected. The guide told them that real knights were roughly the same size as Dad, which was very hard to imagine. Aiden tried on a gauntlet under supervision. His hand barely fit inside.

Aiden and Betty: New Teeth p8: He held it up for a long time. It was tiny. It was the smallest thing that had ever felt significant. He put it in the small envelope Mum left for him, and when he placed it under his pillow it felt ceremonial.
Five Senses p3: I smell with my nose.
Our Five Senses p3: We use our nose to smell.
My Five Senses p4: I can smell. My nose lets me smell nice things.
James and Anna go Shopping p3: The shop was big and bright. It smelled of fresh bread and ripe fruit.
James and Anna go Shopping p5: Anna ran to find the bread. She picked the big round loaf with seeds on top. It smelled amazing!
James and Anna go Shopping p12: On the way home, the bread smelled so good. James started to reach into the bag. Anna gave him a look. He stopped.
Flowers and Trees p4: A rose is red. It smells sweet. It has sharp thorns.

Socks Goes Missing p7: Aiden re-read his field notebook carefully, looking for anything useful. He had written: Drawn to shiny objects. Drawn to warm spots. Will follow interesting smells. Exceptionally good at getting into places he shouldn't. He stared at that last line for quite a long time. It did not narrow things down.

Aiden and Betty and Socks p1: It was an ordinary Saturday in late August when Uncle Eddie arrived at the door with a travelling bag, a very wide smile, and something small and determined making a noise from inside his coat.
James and Anna go Shopping p9: "Can we get a cake each?" asked James. Mum smiled. "One each," she said.
James and Anna go to the Dentist p11: The dentist sang a little tune while she checked Anna's teeth. Anna smiled the whole time. "Ten out of ten!" said the dentist.
James and Anna build a Tree House p14: That night, they got their sleeping bags and climbed up to sleep. Stars shone above them. "Best. Night. Ever," said James. Anna smiled. She agreed.
James and Anna and Chips p12: "CHIPS!" they both said. But they could not stop smiling. Chips was naughty and cheeky and silly — and they loved him so much.
James and Anna's New Bikes p13: That night their legs ached. But they were both smiling. "Same time tomorrow?" said James. Anna was already asleep.
Grumpy Needs Help p8: Thank you, said Grumpy. Not that I needed it. But Grumpy was smiling. Just a little.
My Five Senses p6: I can touch. My hands can feel if things are rough or smooth, hot or cold.

Aiden and Betty go on Holiday p12: Betty found a small smooth white pebble at the shoreline and turned it over and over in her fingers all the way back to the cottage. She was going to put it on her windowsill at home, so she'd always have a piece of the coast — something real from a real day.
Shy's Secret Gift p2: One day it was a smooth pebble for Grumpy. Someone knows I like pebbles, said Grumpy, suspicious but pleased.
James, Anna and Chips go Camping p3: They packed their sleeping bags, a torch, a pack of cards, two books and lots of snacks.
James and Anna visit Grandma's Farm p6: Anna got to brush Bess's mane. James gave her a big red apple. Bess sniffed it — then snaffled the whole thing in one go!
Bugs All Around Us p7: A snail has a shell on its back. A snail moves very slowly.
Bugs All Around Us p7: A snail has a shell on its back. A snail moves very slowly.
Reptiles p3: Snakes are long reptiles.
James and Anna and Chips p7: Then Chips saw a big rose bush. He ran at it. CRUNCH! MUNCH! SNAP! The rose bush was gone.
Sneezy and the Waterfall p11: AAAAAAACHOOOOOO! The sneeze hit the rocks like a wave. Rocks scattered in every direction. The stream burst free. The waterfall roared back. A rainbow appeared.

The Sunny Hollow Games p11: Sneezy entered the farthest-blast competition. Sneezy's sneeze sent the test leaf all the way to the Long Meadow. The judge had to run to collect the result. Zippy collected it. Obviously.
Bouncy Bumps Into Everything p5: Bouncy bumped into Sneezy. Sneezy took a HUGE breath in...
Bouncy Bumps Into Everything p5: Bouncy bumped into Sneezy. Sneezy took a HUGE breath in...
Sneezy and the Waterfall p7: Sneezy had been very quiet. Sneezy could feel something building. High up. In the pollen from the ferns.
Sneezy and the Waterfall p8: Sneezy's nose began to twitch. The Dino Pals turned to look. Oh no, said Bossy. Oh yes, said Wiggly.

The Sunny Hollow Games p11: Sneezy entered the farthest-blast competition. Sneezy's sneeze sent the test leaf all the way to the Long Meadow. The judge had to run to collect the result. Zippy collected it. Obviously.
The Big Storm p5: Sneezy's nose could smell the rain from far away. Twenty minutes, said Sneezy. Maybe fifteen. Everyone moved faster.
James and Anna visit Grandma's Farm p6: Anna got to brush Bess's mane. James gave her a big red apple. Bess sniffed it — then snaffled the whole thing in one go!
Dozy Won't Wake Up p7: Dozy sniffed. Dozy opened one eye. Is that... lunch? said Dozy.
James and Anna go to Space p4: They cut and snipped and stuck and taped. They worked all morning long.
The Four Seasons p7: This is winter. It is cold. Snow falls.
What is Weather? p8: Sun, rain, wind, snow. Weather changes every day. What is the weather today?
Hot and Cold p3: Ice is cold. Snow is cold. Brrr!
Seasons p4: In winter, it snows.
What is Weather? p4: It is snowy. White flakes fall. Everything looks white.
Animals at Night p4: A hedgehog snuffles in the dark. It looks for slugs and bugs.
Animals in the Ocean p8: So many animals live in the sea! The ocean is amazing.
Nan and Bob Learn to Read p6: Bob can read! Bob is so glad!
Fluff Visits the Vet p7: Fluff is so good! What a pup!
Chips's Play Date p6: Bella crashed into the paddling pool. Water exploded in every direction. James and Anna were completely soaked.
Healthy Habits p1: I wash my hands with soap.

Aiden and Betty and Socks p3: Carefully, Uncle Eddie unzipped the carry-case. Inside, curled up like a small comma, was the most compact monkey Aiden had ever seen. He had cream-white fur on his face and chest, darker brown on his back, and small black hands and feet that looked, unmistakably, like he was wearing a pair of extremely neat little socks.

Aiden and Betty and Socks p4: "His name is Socks," said Uncle Eddie. "He was orphaned when he was very small, and I've raised him from a baby. He understands a lot of what you say, he copies behaviour, and he is completely tame." He paused just a moment. "He is also, just occasionally, a little bit naughty."

Aiden and Betty and Socks p5: Socks looked at Aiden. Then he looked at Betty. Then he reached out one small black hand, calmly removed Aiden's watch from Aiden's wrist, and put it on his own. He looked at it with great satisfaction. "Charming," said Mum. "Yes," said Uncle Eddie, completely unsurprised.

Socks Goes Missing p2: Every morning, Socks sat on the kitchen counter and waited while Betty did up the buttons on his little red waistcoat, which always lived on its own peg beside the back door. But on a Tuesday in October, the peg was empty. And when Aiden went to check, Socks's sleeping box in the corner of his room was empty too.
James and Anna and the School Play p4: James practised his dragon roar all week. It shook the windows. Mum wore earmuffs. Chips ran under the sofa.

Socks Goes Missing p3: They searched everywhere. Aiden checked under every sofa cushion and behind every bookshelf. Betty methodically looked in every high cupboard. Mum turned the garden upside down. Dad searched the garden again, more systematically, with a torch — even though it was daytime. Socks was not anywhere.
Baby Animals p1: A baby dog is a pup. A pup is soft and fluffy.
Baby Animals p4: A baby hen is a chick. A chick is soft and yellow.
Baby Animals p5: A baby sheep is a lamb. A lamb has soft wool.

Aiden, Betty and Socks's Big Adventure p7: The far bank was different — thicker trees, softer ground, a field of tall meadow grass beyond the treeline. Socks bounded ahead, turning back every few steps to make sure they were following.
James and Anna visit Grandma's Farm p5: Grandma had a big brown horse called Bess. She was huge and gentle. Her nose was the softest thing James had ever felt.
James and Anna's New Bikes p3: James set off first. He got going — then wobbled — then wobbled more — then crashed softly into the hedge. He was fine. Just surprised.
Shy's Secret Gift p10: Shy! said Sunny, softly. The gifts are from you, aren't they? Shy nodded, going very pink.
Fancy's Bad Day p10: Wiggly dampened the sail gently and pressed it very softly. Slowly, slowly, it began to straighten.
Fancy's Bad Day p6: Dozy offered a pillow. It needs weight, not softness, said Fancy. Dozy went back to sleep.
Little Seeds Grow p2: Put the seed in soil. Pat it down gently.
Little Seeds Grow p4: The sun shines. Something is happening under the soil!
Flowers and Trees p5: Trees have roots under the soil. Roots hold the tree up.

Aiden and Betty and the Science Fair p9: The school hall was full of projects — a volcano that erupted three times, a model solar system, a survey about favourite foods with real graphs. Betty looked at all of them carefully and said nothing. Aiden chewed the inside of his cheek.

Aiden and Betty have a Yard Sale p10: They had set up a lemonade stand beside the sale table — Betty's idea, planned the previous Thursday. Aiden poured from a glass jug. Betty collected the money and kept the queue moving. By one o'clock they had sold every cup and run entirely out of lemons.

Socks Goes Missing p13: Aiden picked him up and held him. Socks was warm and solid and smelled faintly of shortbread. He patted Aiden's cheek twice with one small black hand, the way he sometimes did when he felt like being kind, and then tucked his head under Aiden's chin. Aiden breathed out slowly.

Aiden and Betty Start Grade 1 p4: "In Grade 1," said Miss Okafor, "we will read chapter books, solve number puzzles and begin to understand how the world around us works." Aiden thought that sounded like an awful lot — but he found he was leaning forward slightly without meaning to.
Magnets p2: Magnets pull some metals.
The Four Seasons p8: In winter, some animals sleep. We wrap up warm.
Little Seeds Grow p3: Add some water. The seed drinks it up.

Socks Goes Missing p11: There was Socks, sitting on Mrs Obi's kitchen counter beside an open shortbread tin. He was wearing his waistcoat — which they had also assumed was missing — and had crumbs on his face and the expression of someone who sees absolutely no problem with any of this whatsoever.

Aiden and Betty and the Science Fair p5: Then Socks found the crystal jar. He did not eat it. He simply moved it from the windowsill to a high shelf, where he sat beside it with the expression of someone who had just acquired something important.
Shy's Secret Gift p2: One day it was a smooth pebble for Grumpy. Someone knows I like pebbles, said Grumpy, suspicious but pleased.
Little Seeds Grow p4: The sun shines. Something is happening under the soil!

Aiden and Betty have a Yard Sale p1: It was the first warm Saturday of spring, and Aiden and Betty stood in the middle of the playroom staring at something that had got entirely out of control. Toys, books, games and puzzles were piled up to the shelves. There was even a foam sword that neither of them remembered buying.

Aiden and Betty have a Yard Sale p13: As Aiden folded up the last table, he found something wedged at the very back — his old stuffed elephant, Nelly, who he had completely forgotten was there. He held her for a long moment. Then he carried her inside and put her on the shelf above his desk. Some things, he decided, were not for selling.
The Moon p3: The moon changes shape. Sometimes it looks like a banana.
The Moon p4: Sometimes it is a full circle. This is a full moon.

Socks Goes Missing p13: Aiden picked him up and held him. Socks was warm and solid and smelled faintly of shortbread. He patted Aiden's cheek twice with one small black hand, the way he sometimes did when he felt like being kind, and then tucked his head under Aiden's chin. Aiden breathed out slowly.
James and Anna's New Bikes p9: Anna sat next to the bush. "Same bush as before," she said. James laughed from somewhere inside it.

Aiden and Betty have a Yard Sale p12: They agreed on a proper tent for the garden — not a tiny pop-up one but a real one with a porch and a little vent window. Somewhere they could take their books on rainy afternoons and feel like they were somewhere else entirely.

Aiden and Betty have a Yard Sale p12: They agreed on a proper tent for the garden — not a tiny pop-up one but a real one with a porch and a little vent window. Somewhere they could take their books on rainy afternoons and feel like they were somewhere else entirely.
James and Anna go to Space p13: "Time to go home," said Anna. "Come back soon!" called Zim, and he waved and waved.
Fluff Visits the Vet p1: Fluff has a sore leg.

Aiden and Betty have a Yard Sale p5: On Saturday morning, they dragged two folding tables out to the front path and arranged everything carefully. The old teddies sat in a row at the back and looked slightly sorry for themselves. "They'll go to good homes," said Aiden, mostly to himself, as a form of reassurance.

Aiden and Betty and the Bully p11: A week later, Marcus bumped into Aiden's tray at lunch — accidentally this time, genuinely. He said sorry, just once, with his eyes down. Aiden nodded. He didn't make a big thing of it. Some things, he thought, didn't need to become a story.
Bouncy Bumps Into Everything p3: MY SAIL! cried Fancy. Sorry! called Bouncy, already bouncing away.

Aiden and Betty have a Yard Sale p3: They spent Friday evening sorting everything into three piles: SELL, KEEP and MAYBE. The MAYBE pile kept losing things to SELL, because, as Betty pointed out, they hadn't touched any of them in over a year. By nine o'clock, the MAYBE pile had completely disappeared.

Socks Goes Missing p10: They ran. All four of them, in their socks, without stopping to find shoes, down the pavement to number thirty-seven. They could hear, even from the gate, a small and very determined crunching sound coming from inside.

Aiden, Betty and Socks's Big Adventure p3: The reserve was layered with sounds — insects, birds, the river — all at once and all different. Socks sat on Aiden's shoulder and went completely still, his head turning slowly to track each sound. Aiden had never seen him so quiet.
Aiden and Betty Start Grade 1 p1: The summer holidays were over at last. Aiden sat on his bed and stared at his new school bag. Grade 1. The words sounded bigger than they looked.

Aiden and Betty Start Grade 1 p4: "In Grade 1," said Miss Okafor, "we will read chapter books, solve number puzzles and begin to understand how the world around us works." Aiden thought that sounded like an awful lot — but he found he was leaning forward slightly without meaning to.
My Five Senses p3: I can hear. My ears let me hear sounds.

Aiden, Betty and Socks's Big Adventure p3: The reserve was layered with sounds — insects, birds, the river — all at once and all different. Socks sat on Aiden's shoulder and went completely still, his head turning slowly to track each sound. Aiden had never seen him so quiet.
Aiden and Betty and the Bully p9: He wrote in his notebook that evening: Saying a true thing clearly is harder than it looks and better than it sounds. He underlined it twice.
James and Anna visit Grandma's Farm p11: That evening, Grandma made soup from the garden and fried the eggs James had found. It was the best meal they had ever tasted.

Space p6: Space is big and amazing!
James and Anna go to Space p2: "Let us make a rocket!" said James. "We can fly all the way into space!"
The Big Storm p12: When it passed, Sunny Hollow sparkled. We did that, said Sunny. All of us. Not bad, said Grumpy. From Grumpy, that meant wonderful.
Big and Small p6: Big or small — all things are special! What is big? What is small?

Aiden, Betty and Socks's Big Adventure p4: Betty found the bird hide first — a long wooden structure with narrow slot windows over the reed bed. Inside: a log book, borrowed binoculars, and a board listing twelve species to find. She was immediately in her element.

Aiden, Betty and Socks's Big Adventure p9: Betty wrote down every species she could identify in the hide log book, which she had technically borrowed without permission. Uncle Eddie said they'd bring it back. She wrote in neat columns: dragonflies, water boatmen, a great diving beetle.

Aiden and Betty and the Castle p10: On the way down, Aiden stopped at a narrow slit window in the tower wall — an arrow loop. He looked through it. The angle was exact — a perfect line of sight across the approach. Everything in this building was designed for a specific purpose.
James and Anna visit Grandma's Farm p7: Then Percy the pig got out. He ran in and out of the hen house and round and round the yard at top speed.
Chips's Play Date p5: Bella chased Chips. Chips chased Bella. They ran round and round the garden at an absolutely ridiculous speed.

Aiden and Betty have a Yard Sale p11: "What shall we spend it on?" Betty asked. They sat on the front step with the money tin between them and talked it through the way they talked through important things — slowly and carefully, considering every option.

Aiden and Betty have a Yard Sale p3: They spent Friday evening sorting everything into three piles: SELL, KEEP and MAYBE. The MAYBE pile kept losing things to SELL, because, as Betty pointed out, they hadn't touched any of them in over a year. By nine o'clock, the MAYBE pile had completely disappeared.

Aiden and Betty have a Yard Sale p9: A girl called Rosa arrived late with her grandmother and spent a thoughtful amount of time choosing. She ended up with three books, a snow globe and the foam sword, which she tucked under her arm like a very satisfied general. Aiden watched the sword go and found he didn't mind at all.

Aiden and Betty go on Holiday p6: Aiden spent the whole first morning at a rock pool near the headland. He found three kinds of crab, a sea anemone that closed when you touched it, and a tiny fish he thought might be a blenny. He wrote everything down in his field notebook in careful columns.

Animal Homes p5: A spider spins a web.

Cheeky's Prank Goes Wrong p5: Cheeky laughed and laughed. But then Cheeky slipped on the same mud. SPLAT!
Clumsy to the Rescue p11: Dozy hugged the pillow. Thank you, said Dozy. Both of you. Then Dozy fell asleep, right there, on the spot.
Bugs All Around Us p4: A ladybird is red with black spots. It eats tiny greenfly.
James and Anna go to Space p9: They flew past big round planets. Some had red spots. Some had wide, shining rings!

Aiden and Betty and Socks p8: "He loves shiny objects," Uncle Eddie explained at dinner. "He adores warm spots — the airing cupboard is a particular risk. And if he gets bored, he will find a way to become interesting." Everyone filed this information away carefully.
James and Anna go Shopping p8: Then Anna spotted the cake stand! Big cakes, small cakes, jam tarts and iced buns — all in a long, shining row.
Dozy's Wonderful Dream p9: At the top of Mount Rumble, all the Dino Pals were there. Sitting on the warm peak. Watching all of Sunny Hollow spread out below, tiny and green and perfect.
Seasons p1: In spring, it rains.

Shapes Everywhere p2: A square has four equal sides. A window is a square. A cracker is a square.
What is Flappy? p5: Flappy tried roaring like the other dinosaurs. A small squawk came out. Some feathers drifted down.
The Four Seasons p6: In autumn, animals get ready. A squirrel hides nuts. Birds fly south.
James and Anna and the School Play p3: Anna did not want to be the dragon. She wanted to paint the big backdrop — the scenery behind the stage.
James and Anna and the School Play p8: Then the lights went up and James walked on stage. He could see the WHOLE school in the dark. His legs went very wobbly.
James and Anna and the School Play p12: At the very end, everyone stood and clapped. James and Anna walked to the front of the stage and bowed together.

Aiden and Betty and the Castle p8: The North Tower had one hundred and twelve steps. They counted every one, out loud, all together. The staircase was so narrow they had to go single file. At the top: the whole landscape, the river, the town, a motorway miles away. The same view and entirely different.

Aiden and Betty go on Holiday p1: The last day of the summer term was also the loudest day of the school year. Aiden and Betty burst through the front door and both shouted "HOLIDAY!" at exactly the same moment, which echoed all the way up the stairs and made the dog next door bark.
James and Anna go Shopping p8: Then Anna spotted the cake stand! Big cakes, small cakes, jam tarts and iced buns — all in a long, shining row.

Aiden and Betty have a Yard Sale p10: They had set up a lemonade stand beside the sale table — Betty's idea, planned the previous Thursday. Aiden poured from a glass jug. Betty collected the money and kept the queue moving. By one o'clock they had sold every cup and run entirely out of lemons.

Aiden and Betty and the Castle p9: "Imagine standing here," said Betty quietly, "and not knowing what was coming." Aiden thought about that for a long time, looking out. There was something important in it he couldn't quite hold yet. He wrote it in his notebook anyway, to think about later.
Clumsy to the Rescue p4: Not from where I am standing, said Clumsy. Everyone looked up.
Space p1: The sun is a big star.
Shapes Everywhere p5: A star has five points. We see stars in the night sky.
Hello, Sun! p1: The sun is a big, hot star. It is very far away.
Aiden and Betty Start Grade 1 p1: The summer holidays were over at last. Aiden sat on his bed and stared at his new school bag. Grade 1. The words sounded bigger than they looked.

Socks Goes Missing p7: Aiden re-read his field notebook carefully, looking for anything useful. He had written: Drawn to shiny objects. Drawn to warm spots. Will follow interesting smells. Exceptionally good at getting into places he shouldn't. He stared at that last line for quite a long time. It did not narrow things down.

Aiden and Betty and the Bully p2: Aiden didn't say anything. He went home and sat on his bed and stared at the ceiling. He wasn't crying, but he wasn't fine either. He didn't really want to talk about it — which Betty noticed immediately.
James and Anna build a Tree House p1: At the end of the garden stood a big old oak tree. James and Anna had been staring at it for days.

Aiden and Betty have a Yard Sale p1: It was the first warm Saturday of spring, and Aiden and Betty stood in the middle of the playroom staring at something that had got entirely out of control. Toys, books, games and puzzles were piled up to the shelves. There was even a foam sword that neither of them remembered buying.

Aiden and Betty and the Bully p4: That night, Aiden wrote in his notebook: When something is unfair, you have two choices — pretend it isn't happening, or decide what to do. He starred the second option. He didn't quite know yet what doing something looked like.
Space p4: Stars twinkle in the sky.
Shapes Everywhere p5: A star has five points. We see stars in the night sky.
Day and Night p4: The moon and stars come out. They shine in the dark sky.
James and Anna go Shopping p12: On the way home, the bread smelled so good. James started to reach into the bag. Anna gave him a look. He stopped.

Aiden and Betty Start Grade 1 p12: At home time, Mum was waiting at the gate. She crouched down with an expectant look. "Well?" she said. They both started talking at exactly the same moment and didn't stop for ten minutes.

Aiden and Betty have a Yard Sale p2: "We should have a yard sale," said Betty. She said it the way she said all her best ideas — quickly and firmly, as though the idea had already made up its mind. "We could make actual money." Aiden wasn't certain at first, but when she said the word money, he started to listen.
James, Anna and Chips go Camping p10: The air horn worked. The fox did not come back. Dad DID come — running from the house in his pyjamas, looking very startled.

Socks Goes Missing p12: "How on earth did he get in?" asked Dad. Mrs Obi pointed to the cat flap in her back door. Of course. Socks had worked out the cat flap. He had also — from the state of the shortbread tin — clearly been in before, which nobody had known and which explained certain things about Mrs Obi's biscuit situation.
